mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through higher corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained economic capacity and falling corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or the predictive coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero with p-value above 0.10 in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and stable or falling interest rates indicates accommodative real policy, which compresses equity discount rates and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.1% and FEDFUNDS change <= 0.0
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX forward returns across multiple rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.5%, weak aggregate demand signals impending earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates an efficient economic expansion, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during low-inflation periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over rolling 4-year windows.
